title: Problem Framing
description: Translating a vague client concern into a precise, answerable question before any analysis begins.

title: The Double Diamond
description: A design-thinking process that separates problem exploration from solution development through two rounds of divergent and convergent thinking.

title: Issue Trees & MECE
description: A structured decomposition of any problem into mutually exclusive, collectively exhaustive branches that make complex questions manageable.

title: Hypothesis-Driven Approach
description: Starting with a provisional answer before you have all the data — then proving or disproving it — so analysis is always in service of a decision.

title: Stakeholder Mapping
description: Identifying who has a stake in the outcome, how much influence they hold, and where they stand — so you can build the right coalition for your recommendation.

title: Root-Cause Tools
description: Structured methods for tracing symptoms back to their underlying causes — so the recommendation addresses the real problem, not its visible effects.

title: Scoping the Engagement
description: Defining the boundaries of a consulting engagement before work begins — what's in scope, what isn't, how success will be measured, and what deliverables will look like.

title: SWOT
description: A structured audit of an organization's Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats — the four quadrants that frame any strategic situation assessment.

title: Porter's Five Forces
description: A framework for analyzing the competitive forces that shape industry profitability — revealing whether an industry is structurally attractive and where the power lies.

title: PESTEL
description: A macro-environment audit across Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Environmental, and Legal dimensions — revealing the external forces shaping a market.

title: Value Chain Analysis
description: A systematic breakdown of every activity a firm performs to deliver its product or service — identifying where value is created and where cost advantage or differentiation can be won.

title: BCG Matrix & GE/McKinsey Nine-Box
description: Two portfolio analysis tools for allocating investment across business units — the BCG Growth-Share Matrix and the GE/McKinsey Nine-Box.

title: Ansoff Matrix
description: A 2×2 grid mapping four growth strategies — Market Penetration, Market Development, Product Development, and Diversification — against the risk of entering new markets or creating new products.

title: McKinsey 7S
description: A framework for analyzing organizational alignment across seven interdependent elements — Strategy, Structure, Systems, Shared Values, Style, Staff, and Skills.

title: Marketing Mix (4Ps / 7Ps) & Jobs-to-be-Done
description: Two complementary tools for understanding how a firm creates and delivers value to customers: the Marketing Mix (4Ps / 7Ps) and Jobs-to-be-Done theory.

title: Profit / Profitability Tree
description: A structured decomposition of profit into its revenue and cost drivers — the starting point for any margin analysis, cost reduction, or revenue improvement engagement.

title: Market Sizing & Estimation
description: Techniques for estimating the size of a market or the answer to a quantitative question with limited data — using structured decomposition and Fermi estimation.

title: OKRs
description: A goal-setting framework pairing qualitative Objectives with measurable Key Results — designed to align teams, focus resources, and make progress visible.

title: Balanced Scorecard
description: A performance management framework that translates strategy into objectives and metrics across four perspectives — financial, customer, internal processes, and learning & growth.

title: OGSM
description: A one-page strategy framework connecting Objectives, Goals, Strategies, and Measures — used to align an organization around a clear plan with accountable metrics.

title: Blue Ocean Strategy
description: A strategic theory arguing that lasting competitive advantage comes not from beating rivals but from creating uncontested market space — making the competition irrelevant.

title: Three Horizons
description: A framework for managing a portfolio of strategic initiatives across three time horizons — defending the core today, building emerging growth, and creating options for the future.

title: Scenario Planning
description: A planning discipline that builds multiple plausible stories about how the future might unfold — not to predict which will occur, but to develop strategies robust across multiple possibilities.

title: Wardley Mapping
description: A visual strategy tool that maps the components of a value chain against their stage of evolution — identifying where to invest, where to commoditize, and where competitive advantage lives.

title: Business Model Canvas
description: A one-page visual framework for describing, designing, and analyzing how a business creates, delivers, and captures value — organized across nine building blocks.

title: RACI
description: A responsibility assignment matrix that clarifies who is Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, and Informed for each task or decision — eliminating the ambiguity that causes execution to break down.

title: Kotter's 8 Steps & ADKAR
description: Two complementary change management frameworks — Kotter's 8 Steps at the organizational level and ADKAR at the individual level — for leading change that actually sticks.

title: Lean / Value-Stream Mapping & Six Sigma (DMAIC)
description: Two systematic process improvement methodologies — Lean for eliminating waste and accelerating flow, and Six Sigma / DMAIC for reducing variation and defects.

title: Prioritization
description: Three tools for making defensible decisions about which initiatives to pursue when resources are finite — Impact/Effort Matrix, MoSCoW, and Weighted Scoring.

title: The Pyramid Principle
description: A communication framework that structures any argument so the answer comes first, supported by grouped and summarized reasoning — so the reader never has to wait for the point.

title: Executive-Summary Discipline
description: The discipline of distilling a complex analysis into a single page or slide that contains only what a senior decision-maker needs to act.

title: Storyboarding the Deck
description: The practice of building a presentation's argumentative structure before any slides are created, ensuring the logic holds as a sequence and each page earns its place.

title: The "So What?" Test
description: A synthesis discipline that distinguishes observation from insight by demanding that every finding answer the question 'so what?' — forcing the analyst to complete the logic chain.

title: Client Brief Template
description: A fill-in form for the Client to present a clean, shared problem statement before the Consulting Team begins work.

title: Role Cards
description: One card for each role — Client, Lead Consultant, Analyst, Skeptic, Scribe — with responsibilities and watch-outs.

title: Starter Cases
description: Five ready-to-run practice cases across industries and problem types, for when no one has a live problem to bring.

title: Timekeeper / Agenda Card
description: The 90-minute session structure broken into beats, plus the 15-minute debrief.

title: Debrief & "Would I Hire You?" Scorecard
description: A light rubric across structure, insight, communication, client orientation, and teaming for the 15-minute debrief.

title: Facilitator's Guide
description: How to run a great Consulting Club session: room setup, opening, managing the work phase, the recommendation, the debrief, common failure modes, and closing well.
